feature: Subagent dispatch via the Agent tool
what: The Agent tool (alias Task) launches a specialized subagent as a subprocess with its own context window to handle a complex multi-step task and returns a single result to the parent.
bridge: Codex maps Agent tool_use to the SDK's Agent tool; the parent receives the subagent's final report as the tool result, matching CC's mapToolResultToToolResultBlockParam 'completed' trailer.

what: Built-in and custom agent types are registered as AgentDefinitions with agentType, whenToUse/description, prompt, and capabilities; the model picks one via subagent_type.
bridge: Each CC AgentDefinition becomes one entry in the SDK agents map; whenToUse maps to AgentDefinition.description and the system prompt to AgentDefinition.prompt.

what: An agent definition restricts which tools the subagent may use via tools[] (allow-list, '*' = all) and disallowedTools[] (deny-list, including whole MCP servers).
bridge: Direct field mapping; CC's effective-tools computation (tools minus denySet) is reproduced by the SDK's tools/disallowedTools resolution.

what: Each agent specifies its model: a concrete model id, 'inherit' to reuse the parent's main model, or omitted to fall through to a default subagent model. The Agent tool input also has a model override (sonnet/opus/haiku).
bridge: AgentDefinition.model covers the per-agent model; the Agent tool-input model override (precedence over frontmatter) maps to the SDK Agent tool's input which already accepts a model arg.

what: Agent frontmatter sets per-agent effort (named level low..max or integer) and maxTurns (turn cap), both overriding the parent's values for that subagent's run.
bridge: Direct field mapping; the SDK's per-agent effort (named or integer) and maxTurns are identical in shape to CC's frontmatter fields.

what: An agent runs its tool executions under its own permissionMode (e.g. acceptEdits for workers), overriding the session mode unless the parent is in a more permissive mode.
bridge: Map agent permissionMode directly; the SDK applies it to the subagent's tool executions, matching CC's worker permission-context override.

what: Agent frontmatter lists skills[] to preload into the subagent's context (resolved by exact / plugin-prefixed / suffix-match name).
bridge: Map skills[] directly; SDK preloads the named skills into the agent context as CC does.

what: An agent can declare its own MCP servers (string refs to session servers or inline configs); inline servers are created/cleaned-up per run, string refs are shared.
bridge: Map agent mcpServers to the SDK's per-agent mcpServers spec (string | Record<string,config>); lifecycle of inline-vs-ref servers matches.

what: An agent marked background: true (or invoked with run_in_background) runs async; the parent is notified on completion and must not poll. shouldRunAsync also forced by coordinator/fork/Kairos/proactive.
bridge: Map background:true to AgentDefinition.background; parent observes SDKTaskStarted/SDKTaskNotification messages instead of CC's <task-notification> XML and polls via backgroundTasks().

what: CC ships built-in subagents: general-purpose (tools ['*']), Explore (read-only search, disallows edit tools, haiku/inherit), Plan (architect, inherit), plus statusline-setup and gated verification/claude-code-guide.
sdkSurface: agents map (define each built-in as an AgentDefinition with tools/disallowedTools/model)
bridge: CC's built-ins are not auto-shipped by the SDK; Codex must register equivalents (Explore=read-only disallowedTools, Plan=architect) in the agents map. SDK's preset claude_code may surface some, but the fork-specific set must be re-declared.

what: The model is told to launch multiple agents concurrently via a single message with multiple tool_use blocks to maximize parallelism.
sdkSurface: Agent tool + parent_tool_use_id (multiple concurrent Agent tool_use blocks)
bridge: The SDK already supports multiple concurrent Agent tool calls; parent_tool_use_id correlates each subagent's stream back to its spawning tool_use.

what: A previously spawned agent can be continued by addressing it by agent ID or name; it resumes with full context preserved (CC routes this via SendMessage / resumeAgentBackground).
bridge: SDK exposes subagent transcript retrieval (getSubagentMessages/listSubagents) but resuming a stopped subagent with a new prompt is not a first-class SDK call; Codex reconstructs via session fork/resume plus re-dispatch.

feature: Forward subagent text/thinking to parent stream
what: By default only tool_use/tool_result cross the subagent boundary; CC can surface subagent intermediate output. The SDK gates whether subagent text/thinking blocks are forwarded.
bridge: Set forwardSubagentText:true to stream subagent text/thinking; otherwise only tool_use/result blocks surface, matching CC's default subagent isolation.

feature: Periodic AI progress summaries for running subagents
what: CC runs a 30s-tick fork-based summarizer that emits a human-readable progress summary for each running background subagent (services/AgentSummary).
bridge: Enable agentProgressSummaries:true; the SDK emits task_progress messages carrying the same periodic summary CC generates internally.

what: CC fires SubagentStart hooks (collecting additionalContexts pushed as a user attachment) and clears session hooks on subagent finish.
bridge: Map CC's subagent-start/stop hook firing to the SDK SubagentStart/SubagentStop hook events; additionalContexts become hook-returned context injected into the subagent.

what: An agent definition can carry its own hooks block, registered for the agent's lifetime (gated by plugin-only vs admin-trusted restrictions).
bridge: AgentDefinition in the SDK has no hooks field; per-agent hooks must be emulated by the parent's hooks (matchers scoped by agent) or by Codex wiring agent-scoped hook registration around the SDK call.

what: isolation: 'worktree' runs the subagent in a temporary git worktree (isolated repo copy), auto-cleaned if no changes, with worktree path/branch returned on changes.
sdkSurface: WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove hook events + additionalDirectories (no AgentDefinition.isolation field)
bridge: AgentDefinition has no isolation field in the SDK; Codex creates the worktree and dispatches the agent with cwd/additionalDirectories set, using the worktree hook events for lifecycle. Not a single-flag mapping.

what: ANT-only isolation: 'remote' launches the subagent in a remote CCR sandbox (always background), returning a session URL and output file.
ccSource: spec 14 §6.2 isolation enum (ant); spec 30 §5.14 remote isolation
bridge: Remote-CCR launch is an Anthropic-internal (USER_TYPE=='ant') build-stripped path with no public SDK surface; cannot be reproduced through the Agent SDK.

what: An agent declares memory: 'user'|'project'|'local' to auto-load/persist a per-agentType memory dir; CC also snapshot-syncs project memory.
bridge: Direct field mapping; the SDK auto-loads agent-memory files per scope as CC does. The snapshot-sync confirmation dialog (a TUI-only piece) is the only uncovered part.

what: Under FORK_SUBAGENT, omitting subagent_type forks the current agent: the child inherits the parent's full conversation context (FORK_AGENT, maxTurns 200, model inherit, permissionMode bubble), always async, with recursion guards.
ccSource: spec 14 §3.1 fork routing; spec 30 §5.5 fork path, §6.2 FORK_AGENT
bridge: The SDK Agent tool spawns fresh subagents from a definition; it has no 'fork-self inheriting full live context' primitive. Codex would approximate by passing the parent transcript as forkContextMessages-equivalent prompt, but the no-peek/recursion-guard semantics are bespoke.

what: Custom subagents are markdown files with frontmatter (description, tools, model, isolation, maxTurns, etc.) loaded from settings sources and plugins.
bridge: Markdown-frontmatter agents load via settingSources/plugins; programmatic agents go in the agents map. The frontmatter schema is a superset (isolation, hooks) of AgentDefinition, so a few fields (14.16/14.17) need bridging.

what: Permission rules of the form Agent(type) deny specific agent types; the tool filters candidates by allowedAgentTypes and deny rules and reports denied-vs-nonexistent distinctly.
bridge: Per-agent-type allow/deny is expressible by gating the Agent tool via canUseTool (inspect subagent_type) or by which agents are registered; CC's Agent(type) rule grammar has no exact SDK equivalent so Codex enforces it in the permission callback.

feature: Auto-background a foreground subagent after timeout
what: A foreground sync subagent is auto-demoted to background after getAutoBackgroundMs (default 120s) or an autoBackgroundMs spawn arg, continuing to run off-screen.
bridge: The SDK has explicit background vs foreground via AgentDefinition.background but no automatic foreground-to-background promotion timer; Codex would implement the demotion timer itself if desired.

what: TaskCreate adds a task (subject, description, activeForm, metadata) to a durable, file-backed, per-task-list JSON store with auto-incrementing numeric IDs and a high-water mark.
bridge: The SDK exposes no model-facing TaskCreate tool nor a durable task store; Codex must implement the Task* tool family (or an SDK MCP tool via createSdkMcpServer) backed by its own task store to give the model this surface.

what: TaskUpdate mutates subject/description/activeForm/owner/metadata/status (pending->in_progress->completed, plus deleted) and dependencies; runs TaskCompleted hooks and verification nudges.
bridge: No SDK TaskUpdate tool exists; reproduce as a custom tool. Note the SDK does provide TaskCreated/TaskCompleted hook events that could fire from a Codex-built task tool.

what: TaskGet returns a full task by ID; TaskList returns all non-internal tasks with id/subject/status/owner/blockedBy (filtering completed blockers). Both read-only.
bridge: No SDK equivalent; part of the custom Task* tool family Codex must build on its own task store.

what: TodoWrite is the V1 in-memory per-session/per-agent checklist that rewrites the whole list each call and collapses to [] when all items complete. Disabled by default in favor of Task* V2.
bridge: The SDK does not expose a TodoWrite tool; Codex implements an equivalent session-scoped checklist tool if the model-visible todo surface is wanted.

what: TaskStop (alias KillShell) kills a running background task (shell/agent/etc.) by task_id/shell_id and emits a single-shot SDK terminate event.
sdkSurface: stopTask(taskId) Query method + TaskStop SDK tool + SDKTaskNotification ('stopped')
bridge: Map TaskStop tool_use to the SDK's stopTask() control request; the parent observes the stopped task_notification, mirroring CC's emitTaskTerminatedSdk('stopped').

what: TaskOutput (deprecated; prefer Read on the output file) blocks up to a timeout for a background task to finish, returning status + output/result/error.
sdkSurface: Read tool on the task output file + backgroundTasks() + getSubagentMessages
bridge: CC itself deprecates TaskOutput in favor of Read on the output-file path; the SDK exposes background-task completion via Task* messages and transcript retrieval, so Codex reads the output file / polls backgroundTasks() rather than a dedicated TaskOutput tool.

what: A runtime task registry tracks LocalShellTask/LocalAgentTask/RemoteAgentTask/InProcessTeammateTask/LocalWorkflowTask/MonitorMcpTask/DreamTask with status, output file, notified flag, and per-type kill dispatch.
bridge: The SDK's Task* message stream + backgroundTasks()/stopTask() expose the runtime-task lifecycle CC tracks internally; Codex consumes these instead of reading AppState.tasks directly.

what: Bash run_in_background spawns a LocalShellTask whose output streams to a file and is managed via the task runtime / /tasks.
sdkSurface: Bash tool background mode + SDKTaskStarted/SDKTaskNotification + backgroundTasks()
bridge: The SDK's Bash background execution surfaces as a Task started/notification just like CC's LocalShellTask; the output file is readable via the Read tool.

what: Tasks form a DAG via blocks/blockedBy; claimTask refuses to claim a task whose blockers are unresolved, and TaskList drops already-completed blockers.
bridge: Dependency graph semantics live entirely in CC's task store; no SDK surface. Part of the custom Task* tool family Codex would build.

what: Tasks carry an owner (agent name); moving to in_progress on a swarm auto-claims for the calling agent, and owner changes notify the assignee via mailbox.
bridge: Task ownership/claim is swarm-coordination logic over CC's task store with no SDK equivalent; depends on the multi-agent mailbox (30 cluster). Custom build.

what: TaskCreate runs TaskCreated hooks (rolling back the task on a blocking error) and TaskUpdate runs TaskCompleted hooks on completion (blocking the transition on error).
bridge: The SDK exposes TaskCreated/TaskCompleted hook events; a Codex-built task tool fires them, and hook blocking-error semantics map to a deny/abort hook decision.

what: On main-thread completion of all (>=3) tasks with no 'verif' subject, TaskUpdate/TodoWrite set verificationNudgeNeeded and append a reminder to spawn a verification agent.
bridge: Gated on the internal VERIFICATION_AGENT feature + tengu_hive_evidence flag; no SDK surface. Codex could emulate via a Stop/TaskCompleted hook that injects the nudge.

what: DreamTask is a background fork that consolidates memory across sessions under time/session/lock gates; surfaces an inline system message on success.
bridge: Auto-dream is a KAIROS/RemoteMode-gated internal consolidation fork tied to CC's memory subsystem; it is not exposed by the Agent SDK (memory consolidation belongs to a separate, non-public service).

what: When Agent is called with both team_name and name, spawnTeammate launches a peer Claude teammate via tmux / iTerm2 / in-process backend, registers it in the team, and delivers the initial prompt via mailbox.
bridge: The SDK's agents/Agent tool spawns child subagents, not peer terminal-pane Claude sessions; the multi-process tmux/iTerm2 teammate model has no SDK equivalent. Codex would orchestrate peers by spawning multiple SDK queries and coordinating them itself.

feature: Create / disband an agent team (TeamCreate / TeamDelete)
what: TeamCreate sets up a team (config file, shared task list, lead agent id, AppState.teamContext); TeamDelete cleans up team/task dirs and worktrees, refusing if active members remain.
bridge: No SDK tools create/disband a multi-agent team; this is experimental CLI-gated (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS) swarm infrastructure. Codex builds team + shared-task-list coordination on top of multiple SDK sessions.

what: SendMessage routes a plain-text or structured message to a teammate by name, '*' broadcast, or a peer over uds/bridge; also continues/resumes a subagent by agent id.
bridge: Inter-agent mailbox messaging is bespoke swarm transport (file inbox + uds/bridge) with no SDK equivalent; the closest SDK affordance is streamInput for one query. Codex implements its own message bus for peers.

what: COORDINATOR_MODE turns the main thread into an orchestrator: a coordinator system prompt, a filtered tool pool (Agent/TaskStop/SendMessage/SyntheticOutput), a rewritten built-in agent list, and every Agent call forced async.
bridge: The coordinator persona is a CC-internal system-prompt + tool-whitelist preset (feature-gated); the SDK has no coordinator mode. Codex would assemble an equivalent system prompt and restrict tools via the agents map / disallowedTools to emulate orchestration.

what: A swarm worker short-circuits its own permission prompt and round-trips permission_request/permission_response envelopes to the leader's inbox, which surfaces the dialog and writes back the decision.
bridge: The worker-to-leader permission RPC over the mailbox is swarm-specific; the SDK's canUseTool runs locally per query. Codex could centralize permissions by having one process answer canUseTool for all peers, but the mailbox RPC itself is bespoke.

what: A teammate that finishes its work signals idle via an idle_notification envelope; the leader is notified a teammate is awaiting more work.
ccSource: spec 30 §4.5 mailbox (idle_notification); TeamCreate prompt 'Teammate Idle State'
bridge: The SDK exposes a TeammateIdle hook event that fires when a teammate becomes idle, covering the notification semantics CC delivers over the mailbox idle envelope.

what: SendMessage carries structured envelopes for graceful shutdown (shutdown_request/approved/rejected) and plan-mode approval (plan_approval_request/response) routed through the leader.
bridge: These handshakes ride on the bespoke SendMessage/mailbox transport (30.3) with no SDK equivalent (the SDK has per-query plan mode but not a cross-agent approval handshake). Codex builds the handshakes over its message bus.

what: Completed/failed/killed async agents return a <task-notification> XML envelope (task-id, status, summary, result, usage) that re-enters the parent/coordinator turn.
bridge: The SDK delivers settled-task info as structured Task* messages rather than XML text; Codex consumes the message fields (status/summary/result/usage) directly instead of parsing the envelope.

what: resolveTeammateModel resolves a teammate's model: 'inherit' uses the leader's model, undefined falls to teammateDefaultModel or a hardcoded fallback.
bridge: Per-teammate model selection maps to AgentDefinition.model / the per-agent model arg; the inherit/default fallback chain is reproduced by Codex choosing the model when registering each agent.

what: Each subagent persists its own sidechain transcript and metadata (agentType, worktreePath, description), enabling later resume and per-subagent message retrieval.
bridge: The SDK persists subagent transcripts and exposes them via getSubagentMessages/listSubagents, covering CC's sidechain-transcript persistence (resume detail in 14.12).

what: applyCoordinatorToolFilter restricts the coordinator's tool pool to Agent/TaskStop/SendMessage/SyntheticOutput, with INTERNAL_WORKER_TOOLS removed, applied identically on REPL and headless paths.
bridge: Restricting the orchestrator to a small tool whitelist is expressible via the SDK's tools/allowedTools/disallowedTools; the specific coordinator whitelist (incl. SyntheticOutput) is a Codex policy choice layered on those fields.

what: The swarm subsystem spawns teammates through tmux, iTerm2 (it2), or in-process pane backends behind a common executor, with detection and reconnection.
bridge: Terminal-multiplexer pane spawning is a CC TUI-runtime concern with no SDK surface; the Agent SDK runs subagents in-process within the CLI subprocess. Out of scope for an SDK-based reimplementation.

feature: Swarm view UI (panes, agent dialog, status pill)
what: The multi-agent swarm UI (~13 components: swarm view layout, teammate panes, agent dialogs, status pills, it2-setup prompt, color assignment) renders and manages running teammates.
bridge: All swarm UI rendering is owned by cluster C11 (UI shell); the SDK provides no UI. Coordination state (this cluster) is separate from its visualization.

what: On resume, periodic summarization is enabled when coordinator OR fork-subagent OR the SDK agentProgressSummaries flag is on, generating per-agent progress summaries.
bridge: The exact condition CC checks includes getSdkAgentProgressSummariesEnabled(), confirming the SDK flag drives the same summarization path; map directly.

what: Subagent messages and task progress are correlated back to the spawning Agent tool_use so the parent can attribute concurrent subagents' output and results.
bridge: The SDK tags subagent and task-progress messages with parent_tool_use_id, giving Codex the same attribution CC derives from the spawning tool_use id.
